title: Check-In Response
description: The canonical shape of a CheckInResponse record — docs/44-PHASE-2B-TECHNICAL-PLAN.md §10.3/§11.4's Daily Check-in design, batch PXP-5 (docs/44 §22), governed by docs/47-PHASE-2B-IMPLEMENTATION-RULES.md. The designed successor to symptom-log.schema.json, shipped alongside it, never replacing it in this batch (docs/44 §10.1). `answers` is this schema's one disclosed exception to ADR-006's flat-column convention: stored as a JSON-encoded string in the underlying Sheet cell (docs/44 §11.4), but this schema — the entity's real, contractual shape (shared/README.md's 'Contract vs. implementation-only detail') — describes it as a real object, since apps-script/CheckInResponse.gs's own foundationCheckInRowToApiShape_() always parses it back before returning a record to any caller. Create and list only: no update, no delete, no per-entry get-by-id, the same lifecycle symptom-log.schema.json already establishes. Both template_id and template_version are stored together, permanently pinning the exact question set that produced this row's answers even after the template is later edited into a newer version (docs/44 §11.4).

record_id.description: Server-generated UUID (generateFoundationId_()), this row's own identity — never patient-supplied.

patient_id.description: The Patient Identity this response belongs to (docs/33 §1.2, ADR-002). Always session-derived — never client-supplied.

template_id.description: The CheckInTemplate this response answers, per shared/constants/template-registry.json. Paired with template_version below — pinning both, not just this field, is what keeps this row permanently interpretable even after the template is later edited (docs/44 §11.4).

template_version.description: The exact, immutable Template Registry version this response was recorded against (docs/44 §11.2/§11.4). Never re-resolved to a later version after the fact — an existing row is never rewritten to match a newer template version.

answers.description: A flat object of {field_key: value} pairs, one per question the referenced (template_id, template_version) declares that the patient actually answered (docs/44 §11.4: no nested objects or arrays in version 1 of this policy). Keys are written in the template's own question-list order at write time (apps-script/CheckInResponse.gs's foundationBuildDeterministicCheckInAnswers_()) so re-serializing identical answers never produces a spurious byte-level diff. Stored as a JSON-encoded string in the underlying Sheet cell — an implementation-only detail, not part of this contract (shared/README.md); every function returning a record to a caller parses it back into this real object shape first.

condition_slug.description: Optional metadata only, for reporting/filtering — never an authorization or assignment mechanism (docs/44 §10.3: assignment authority is the doctor's own CheckInTemplateAssignment action, not this field). Empty-string sentinel when not provided, mirroring symptom-log.schema.json's own condition_slug convention exactly.
